program_name: Applied Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence
campus: Città Universitaria, Rome (ACSAI office at Viale Regina Elena 295)
faculty: Faculty of Information Engineering, Informatics and Statistics (Ingegneria dell'Informazione, Informatica e Statistica)
admission_test_type: TOLC-I (English TOLC-I, CISIA) or SAT General Test (College Board)
applicant_route: non-EU residing abroad (visa-required, second selection round only)
application_platform: Sapienza selection registration (Infostud) plus CISIA (TOLC-I) or College Board (SAT) test registration
application_round: Second selection (open to non-EU residing abroad)
places_and_foreign_contingent: 180 places total. Three selection rounds; unfilled places roll over to the next round. The second selection round is open to non-EU citizens residing abroad. Per-round and non-EU contingent breakdown [uncertain].

academic_requirements.secondary_school_diploma: A secondary-school diploma giving access to higher education is required.
academic_requirements.non_eu_requirements: Non-EU applicants residing abroad must meet the Sapienza academic requirements (MoveIN 2026-2027) and obtain a study visa. [uncertain]
academic_requirements.test_requirement: Admission is by selection based on the English TOLC-I (CISIA) or SAT General Test.

country_specific_eligibility: Country-specific qualification and minimum schooling requirements per the Sapienza academic requirements document (MoveIN 2026-2027). [uncertain]

language_requirements.level: Minimum B2 (CEFR) English required to take part in the selection.
language_requirements.exemption: Candidates who sit the English TOLC-I (including TOLC@HOME) or the SAT are exempted from submitting an English language certificate, as English knowledge is automatically verified.
language_requirements.minimum_scores: SAT 960/1600, or English TOLC-I (CISIA) 36/50 satisfies the English requirement.
language_requirements.accepted_certificates: If not exempt: FCE, IELTS (academic), TOEFL iBT, CAE, CPE, PTE (B2 or higher).

language_submission_timing: At/through the admission test; otherwise by enrolment via certificate or university online interview. [uncertain]
application_deadline_eu: EU and equivalent candidates (no visa required): on or before 31 July 2026; test/registration/fee must be completed by the deadline set for each selection round. [uncertain on per-round dates]
application_deadline_non_eu: Non-EU residing abroad (visa-required): MoveIN online pre-selection by 15 May 2026; Universitaly pre-enrolment by 30 June 2026; ACSAI second selection round applies. [uncertain on per-round dates]
application_fee: €30 per application (standard Sapienza fee; max 2 applications per intake). [uncertain]
visa_preenrolment_deadline: Universitaly pre-enrolment by 30 June 2026 for non-EU visa-required applicants.
required_documents: Secondary-school diploma / qualification giving access to higher education
English TOLC-I or SAT score (or English language certificate if not exempt)
For non-EU residing abroad: documents required by MoveIN pre-selection and Universitaly pre-enrolment (visa)
document_translation_authentication: Foreign qualifications typically require Declaration of Value or CIMEA Statement of Comparability plus translation/legalisation. [uncertain]

entry_exam_or_test.type: English TOLC-I (CISIA) or SAT General Test (College Board)
entry_exam_or_test.mode: TOLC-I available in TOLC@HOME (online) and in-person formats
entry_exam_or_test.registration: Register for the test with CISIA/College Board and for the Sapienza selection; complete test, registration and fee by each selection round deadline under penalty of exclusion.

test_variant_and_session: English TOLC-I (TOLC@HOME or in-person) or SAT. Specific 2026-2027 session dates per selection round [uncertain].
test_score_conversion_or_threshold: Ranking-based selection. English requirement satisfied at SAT 960/1600 or English TOLC-I 36/50. Admission cutoff depends on ranking per round. [uncertain]

source_quotes.quote: Applied Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence is a restricted-access programme with 180 places available. Three selections are foreseen, and any places not filled in one selection will be made available for the next selection.
To gain admittance to the degree programme, you can choose between TOLC-I (Online Test of CISIA – Engineering) or SAT (Scholastic Assessment Test).

Candidates who undergo the English TOLC-I (also in the TOLC@HOME format) or SAT are exempted from submitting an English Language Certificate ... a SAT test score of 960 out of 1600, or an English TOLC-I (CISIA) score of 36 out of 50.
the second selection round is open to non-EU citizens who are residing abroad, who ... are required to obtain a visa for study purposes.

uncertainty_notes: Exact per-selection-round dates for 2026-2027 not extracted; cross-cutting MoveIN (15 May 2026), Universitaly (30 June 2026) and EU (31 July 2026) deadlines applied from the central 2026-2027 admissions sources.
Non-EU reserved quota for ACSAI not extracted from the programme Call PDF.
Application fee (€30) is the standard Sapienza fee; not separately confirmed for ACSAI 2026-2027.
MoveIN pre-selection requirement and intake semester inferred from general Sapienza non-EU procedure, not confirmed specifically for ACSAI.
Document authentication (DoV/CIMEA) inferred from general Sapienza policy.
